name: Republic of Korea Army (ROKA)
detail: Active strength: ~365,000 personnel. Equipped with K2 Black Panther tanks (560+), K9 Thunder self-propelled howitzers, and Black Hawk helicopters. Maintains 3 Army HQs, 11 corps, and 40+ divisions. Universal male conscription (18-21 months). Responsible for defending the Korean DMZ alongside US Forces Korea.

name: Republic of Korea Navy (ROKN)
detail: Active strength: ~70,000 personnel. Fleet includes 3 King Sejong the Great-class Aegis destroyers, 12 Chungmugong Yi Sun-sin-class destroyers, 18 submarines (Type 214/209), and the Dokdo-class amphibious assault ship. The ROK Marine Corps (29,000) operates as part of the Navy. Conducts regular exercises with US Navy (Carrier Strike Group deployments).

name: Republic of Korea Air Force (ROKAF)
detail: Active strength: ~65,000 personnel. Operates 40+ F-35A stealth fighters (deliveries 2019-2021), 60 F-15Ks, and 170+ KF-16s. Developing the KF-21 Boramae 4.5-generation domestic fighter (first flight April 2023). The ROKAF is transitioning to 5th-generation capability. Maintains 10+ major air bases including Osan AB and Gunsan AB hosting US airpower.

name: US Forces Korea (USFK)
detail: Approximately 28,500 US military personnel stationed in South Korea under the 1953 Mutual Defense Treaty. Headquartered at Camp Humphreys, Pyeongtaek (since 2018). Includes the 2nd Infantry Division, 7th Air Force (F-16s at Osan, F-16s at Kunsan), and Naval Forces Korea. Conducts Freedom Shield and Ulchi Freedom Shield annual exercises with ROK forces.

name: THAAD Battery — Seongju, North Gyeongsang Province
detail: Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) battery deployed at a former golf course in Seongju county since 2017. Designed to intercept North Korean ballistic missiles in the terminal phase. The AN/TPY-2 X-band radar provides detection of incoming missiles at long range. Deployment triggered severe Chinese economic retaliation (2017) and remains a geopolitical flashpoint in Korea-China relations.

name: KF-21 Boramae Fighter Development
detail: South Korea's KAI KF-21 Boramae is a domestically-developed 4.5th-generation multirole fighter developed jointly with Indonesia (30% cost share). First flight: April 19, 2022. Program aims for IOC in 2026 with initial production of 120 aircraft. Unit cost approximately $65 million. Features AESA radar, internal weapons bay (future Block III), and supersonic dash capability.
